seed_problem: Themed grid network; remove at most k links to maximize the increase in the s-t shortest path; checker verifies budget + connectivity and scores achieved delay.
mutation: goal+output: generalize the single s-t shortest-path interdiction to MULTI-TERMINAL interdiction -- maximize the SUM of shortest-path costs from one hive source to a set of q forage-patch terminals, subject to keeping every terminal reachable. The grid is overlaid with sparse cheap 'highway' shortcuts that carry most traffic, so budget-k destruction is NP-hard and greedy vs. best-improvement local search diverge.
title: Wasp Blight in the Apiary: Foraging-Corridor Interdiction
strategies: do-nothing baseline: destroy no corridors (achieves the original total foraging effort)
traffic-weighted greedy: build one shortest-path tree, weight each edge by (terminal-traffic x cost), destroy the top-k that keep all patches reachable, no recomputation

best-improvement local search: each round recompute all shortest paths, evaluate the true objective gain of destroying each shortest-path-tree edge, commit the single best feasible one, repeat k times
randomized multi-restart: perturb the greedy ranking / tie-breaks across restarts and keep the interdiction set that maximizes final total effort
